captured.evidence: vessel-rich group gets 75% of CO despite being 10% of body weight
drug moves out of brain into muscle and fat; hepatic metabolism too slow to explain 8-min awakening
time for plasma level to fall by half after stopping an infusion of a given length gets longer the longer the infusion ran
after 4 hours muscle and fat are filled, brain level only drops via slow hepatic metabolism
lungs buffer peak arterial concentration; fentanyl 65-75% first-pass pulmonary uptake

summary: Complete and mechanistically accurate. The student correctly distinguished redistribution-driven awakening from metabolism-driven emergence, explained the context-sensitive half-time concept in their own words, and addressed pulmonary first-pass uptake and geriatric dose reduction. Nothing to fix.
voss_quip: Fine. You actually understand why the bolus patient wakes up — most of your classmates blame the liver.
